Right now what we're going to move into is we're going to move into webinars and how I kind of do them for the invisible funnel compared to how he does them for the invisible funnel and it should be a pretty cool topic. But the purpose of it is just so they love it. So it's a lot of fun. They feel like they leave with a lot of value. But it's not like, I don't think about it like we would think about a regular product. It's an experience. So all of our launches, when it comes to webinars, it's always, we send them to the same opt-in page, but we change the video every single time. So they go back and if they don't sign up the first time, well, they go back again to see what you're talking about today. And then they might sign up on the third time. So we go from our first email getting maybe six, seven hundred signups and then we'll get another 400, another 400, another, you know, 300, another 400. So, and the people that didn't see, and here's the big, the other big thing that we noticed is that if you're emailing one day and it's got one hook and that hook didn't work too well, then throw another hook at them with another video. So one day we might get 300 signups and then we throw in another hook and then another gang of people that are seeing it are like, oh, I didn't see this yesterday. That's a pretty crazy hook. So where was I going with this? Where I was going with all this? So that's Roger. That's who was just speaking. Whenever I put together an offer, I want to make sure that I can prove. I have proof that comes before I even present the offer. So if I'm going to say, we're going to teach you how to create a list that can produce 330 clicks per day, I want them to already know mentally how much money that's going to mean to them. So I don't have to tell them what that's going to put in their pocketbook or put in their pocket or their wallet. They'll just say, I can't wait to get there. And so for our market, I think a $10,000 per month point for a beginner is it'd be awesome to get to $10,000. And that's pretty much why I chose that number of clicks. And so one of the things that I'll do is I'll go through, like this is just me demonstrating proof, is we'll go through, if you're doing ad swaps and you have the right people to do ad swaps in the content event, here's how quickly your list can grow. And so if your list is going by 100 people per day and you're doing an ad swap with someone that's got a list of the same size, here's what that map plays out to be. They get a real tangible feel for what this business is going to be like once they're in it. And so I'm always very mindful of things like that. We brought Carlos on when we did that webinar to give another type of proof because he was actually running a list publishing business and we wanted them to see that it was no work. So he came on and showed what he was doing in his business and also that literally you paid your brother like, how much? $500 a month to make you $20,000 a month pretty passively. So anything I can do, that's why this is such an awesome business. Look at Carlos' business, here's the real numbers, it's awesome. Well, you said it's not going to be as good, but that's what I'm more about. I just think this is very freeing, as someone that's super, like, not techy at all, pretty new to the online space of this last year, I literally did my first webinar last week, but I'll get on and hang out in a minute, I can do screen share stuff, I get that, or just talking and training, do slides and all this stuff, it's always been like, what's kind of held me back, so it's freeing to do that and deliver content, and I'm already pretty good at working with, and yet it can do very well. What I think is interesting is, like, you look at most of us, like, got in this business because we were passionate about teaching our thing, and we had to learn how to become salespeople, where this is like, get rid of, like, how to become a salesperson, just do your passion and everything else will just make sense, it all falls into place. It's really cool. I think it does, like, the person with literally the most value wins, you know what I mean? It's not the person that actually sells the best. I want to add one more thing, though, to this. So one thing, and this is something I kind of got, again, from the whole CHET model, is when you're transitioning from the story before the offer, I think this is, like, the critical key point that I always try to focus on. If you remember, I talked about it on CHET's, at the end of it, when they come back, and they're like, hey, Mike, so how much was this webinar worth to you in the next year? How much was this? How much was this? I'm always big on that. After I've gone through everything, it's trying to help them establish their value in their head of what that actually was worth to them. And as soon as they've got that, as soon as you have that magic number, then you, because you almost have two pitches here. Like, the first pitch is, come to bill your card for the $97, and then you're going to pitch the next thing, and they're two separate things, but I think you have to have that distinct break. Otherwise, the offer doesn't work, because if you go straight to the offer, then they're like, wait a minute, I was here for $97, now you're pitching me? But if you have this distinct break, we say, what was the value to you? Boom is worth $97, worth $500 worth of million. Cool, we know now. Okay, so now because of that, now we're going to end this coaching part of the game. Tomorrow we're going to bill you, or two days from now, we're going to bill you the $97. Just look for that, for whatever reason, you come into your pitch of how to cancel if you're not interested. And then when that's over, then in their mind, what I paid for is over now. Now we transition. Now let me show you this really cool offer. I'm one of those who's still interested and want something more. Let me show you this cool thing we've got. And then it just takes away, there's no complaints at that point. No, I just ask them, would you guys like me to show you a special opportunity, and I'll write that in Facebook, and they'll see like 80, 100 different people say yes, yes, yes, please. All right, here it is. You kind of mentally complete that, then even use the Facebook thread to get that kind of like, validation, conformation, and how you want to learn more, so you get that permission in the sense to then move on to your... And then you have nobody that's angry. I've seen a couple people do it where they just run right from this all the way to this, and they get pissed off people, holding flops, but that breakage I think is the key to really solidifying the sale, making sure they remember, and then moving on. Yeah. If you don't want to use Facebook, what's the need for it also is you can use the polling and vote together. Yeah. And I've done that many times, so you get your answer to people, and you just like, you count down the stack, I need to, you need to answer this, I'm waiting for you five, four, and you get people, and then they answer, and then you get a lot of the same benefit from it, you don't want to use Facebook. Yeah. I just, I like Facebook. But there's lots of ways. The point is just to interact. I mean, you know what I mean? Like, I think a lot of times with webinars, we think we're just like talking at them. I'm sitting and watching, but... I want to engage. I want to be as engaged, and I want them to engage as much as possible. If you look at the levels, you look like Chet's model. Yes. I'd like to hear what more you have. Have you guys found your Brussels, your done-free offer? Is there a benefit to doing break-ins before the offer? I've always done it before just because I've, for me, I like that. I like the separation of it because I hate, I just think if I was on something and at the end of it, I saw a pitch, I'd be like, this is the exact same thing on every webinar I've been on. I go to a webinar, but now I have to pay for it. Whereas if I have that breakage before I switch to offer. At that point, someone's been on for four hours with you. You don't see all of a sudden a huge drop. Most of them are concerned like, Yeah, or have it wrong, but most of that point are concerned like, am I going to get billed? What's going to be happening? That's why I think why I love and wait for four hours. Then I kind of clear all that up. I want to get all those thoughts out of their head before I move to the offer. I want to clear this so it happens at the end of it. Okay, you guys all cool with that? Now, boom, and I bring on the next thing. And I haven't seen big drop-offs during that part, but... One thing I do want to share that I didn't share closely is like, when I do the content webinar, I definitely want to take people down a logical chain of beliefs that they need to have in order to buy. But I'm pretty... I like to write them out. So I have like belief one, two, three, four, five. I have a problem. It might be the first logical belief. Other people are successful. If I fix my problem, then I can also be successful. Number two, number three, et cetera, as it relates to your product. But most people, like their perception is like, it's going to be an uphill battle to get that stuff taken care of. So when I'm giving content, I always try to make it a downhill battle. What most people will tell you to do is you got to go create a product. But man, if I was starting over, I wouldn't. Because in this marketplace, people don't just want to buy your cool thing. They want money. And they don't want a little bit of money. They want like five streams of income. They want multiple streams of income. It's a free website called Plugging Profits. Just go over there and get that thing. People join in all day. Product done. You see? So then they're like, awesome, awesome, awesome. And it just gets them engaged even more. So I try to make sure if I can, if I've got it clear in my head, if something's going to be perceived as tough, how can I give them an easier way out? Because it's only going to help at the end. We just record it as knowing it's going to be automated. And then the way the software works is really cool. You upload your video, it pops out a little box that you can embed on your thank you page, or it also will create a registration in a thank you page for you, depending on how techy you are. And then someone comes in, they register, boom, after they register, you can have email sequences based on anything to do. They register and they don't show up, you can have emails pushing to replay if they do show up. If they leave early, you can have pushing to replay. You can do all sorts of really cool, easy segmenting inside of the software, getting them to come and do stuff. You can have warnings, I mean, I'm going to bill you tomorrow, we're billing you in 24 hours, 12 hours, any kind of stuff you want to do, you can build out really simply and easily.
